gift.mail.body.reciprocal.caroline.2: Dear @,^The {{sourceGift}} was a lovely surprise. It brought a calm little brightness into the day.^I sent {{item}} for you. I hope it adds something pleasant to yours.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.carol: Dear @,^Thank you for helping with {{sourceGift}}. The relief of having something taken care of can be its own kind of quiet joy.^Please take {{item}} with my gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.demetrius.2: Hello @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. I noted a measurable improvement in the day after receiving it, which is a formal way of saying I appreciated it.^I sent {{item}}. It may be useful in your work.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.demet: Hello @,^Your delivery of {{sourceGift}} resolved the request cleanly. Efficient assistance is worth recognizing.^I mailed {{item}} as thanks. I hope its utility is apparent.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.jodi.2: Hi @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. In the middle of a busy household, being thought of like that really stands out.^I sent {{item}}. I hope it makes one of your days a little easier.
Hi @,^You bringing {{sourceGift}} helped more than you might think. One less worry can change the whole shape of a day.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.kent.2: @,^I wanted to thank you for {{sourceGift}}. I am still relearning how to answer kindness without overthinking it.^I sent {{item}}. It seemed like a steady, useful thing to give back.
@,^You followed through on {{sourceGift}}. Reliable help means something to me.^Please take {{item}}. It is a simple thank-you, but a sincere one.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.lewis.2: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. Gestures like that are what make Pelican Town feel like a proper community.^I sent {{item}} as a small return. Please accept my appreciation.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.lewis: Dear @,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} was much appreciated. A town runs better when people look after each other.^I mailed {{item}} as thanks for doing your part so kindly.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sandy.2: Hello sweetie,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was such a welcome little sparkle out here.^I sent {{item}} your way. Think of it as a desert hello tucked into the mail.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.sandy: Hello sweetie,^You brought {{sourceGift}}, and I really appreciate you making the effort.^I mailed {{item}} as thanks. I hope it adds a bit of brightness to your day.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.dwarf.2: @ friend,^Your {{sourceGift}} was received and categorized as a successful friendship exchange.^I send {{item}}. Its value is practical, symbolic, and probably acceptable.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.dwarf: Surface friend,^You completed the {{sourceGift}} request with good efficiency. This is noted favorably.^Please accept {{item}}. It is a reward, not a trade demand.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.leo.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It felt like finding a good thing in a new place and realizing someone meant you to have it.^I sent {{item}}. I hope it feels friendly too.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.leo.2: @,^You remembered {{sourceGift}}, and that made the valley feel easier to understand.^Please take {{item}}. I picked it because I wanted to answer your kindness.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.vincent.2: Hi @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}! I liked it a lot and wanted to send something back like grown-ups do.^Here is {{item}}. I hope you like it too!
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.vince: Hi @,^You brought {{sourceGift}} like you promised. That was really nice.^I sent {{item}} because helpers should get thank-you presents!
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.jas.2: Hi @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was kind of you, and I remembered it after you left.^I sent {{item}}. I hope that is a good thank-you.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.jas.2: Hi @,^Thank you for bringing {{sourceGift}}. I was happy that you remembered.^Please take {{item}}. I picked it for you.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.andy.2: @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. I will admit, a decent neighborly gesture still counts for something these days.^I sent {{item}}. It is practical, which means it is worth having.
@,^You brought {{sourceGift}} just like you said. That is the kind of follow-through a farm runs on.^I mailed {{item}} as thanks. Put it to good use.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.claire.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was a small bright thing in a long day, and I appreciated it more once everything quieted down.^I sent {{item}}. I hope it reaches you at a better hour.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.clair: @,^Thanks for helping with {{sourceGift}}. You made one part of the day feel handled instead of hanging over me.^Please accept {{item}} with my quiet gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.sophia.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. I got nervous about how to answer, but it really did make me happy.^I sent {{item}} for you. I hope it feels gentle and useful.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.sophi: @,^You remembered {{sourceGift}}, and that meant more than I expected. It made the whole request feel less lonely.^Please accept {{item}}. I chose it with care.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.susan.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. One farmer looking out for another still means a great deal to me.^I sent {{item}}. Hope it earns its keep on your farm.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.susan: @,^You helped with {{sourceGift}}, and I am grateful. A reliable neighbor can make a hard season feel manageable.^Please take {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.victor.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It was a thoughtful gesture with good structure, if that is not too odd a way to put it.^I sent {{item}}. I hope it fits neatly into your day.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.victo: @,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} was timely and well considered. I appreciate both parts.^Please accept {{item}} as a small but sincere thank-you.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.olivia.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It arrived with a kind of understated grace I found very charming.^I sent {{item}} in return. Please receive it with my warm regards.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.olivi: @,^You handled {{sourceGift}} with admirable thoughtfulness. I appreciate being able to rely on someone without making a production of it.^Please accept {{item}} as my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.lance.2: Farmer,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. Useful provisions and timely kindness can both change the course of a day.^I sent {{item}} in return. Carry it well if it proves useful.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.lance: Farmer,^You brought {{sourceGift}} as requested. Clean execution is rare enough to deserve notice.^I mailed {{item}} with my thanks. May it serve you when needed.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.scarlett.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}! I tried to be casual about it, but it absolutely made me smile.^I sent {{item}} back to you. Hope it gives your day a little lift.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.scarl: @,^You helped with {{sourceGift}}, and I am still grateful. You made the whole thing feel lighter.^Please accept {{item}}. I sent it with all the enthusiasm a mailbox can handle.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.gunther.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. Objects passed between people often gather meaning, and this one arrived with a kind story attached.^I sent {{item}} in return. May it find a useful place with you.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.gunth: @,^Your assistance with {{sourceGift}} helped put a small matter back where it belonged.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks. Reliable help is worth preserving.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.martin.2: @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. It made an ordinary workday feel a lot less ordinary.^I sent {{item}} back. I hope it is the kind of thing you can actually use!
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.marti: @,^You brought {{sourceGift}}, and that was honestly a big help.^Please take {{item}}. I am still figuring out thank-you letters, but I really mean this one.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.morris.2: @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. Courtesy outside a formal transaction is inefficient in the best possible way.^I sent {{item}} as a return gesture. Please accept my regards.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.morri: @,^Your help with {{sourceGift}} resolved the matter with minimal friction. That is appreciated.^I mailed {{item}} as compensation and thanks for your time.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.leah.2: Dear @,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. It felt intentional, like something chosen by hand instead of chance.^I sent {{item}} in return. I hope it adds a little texture to your day.
Dear @,^Because you brought {{sourceGift}}, I was able to finish what had been waiting. There is a quiet beauty in help that simply arrives.^Please accept {{item}} with my thanks.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.elliott.2: Dear @,^The {{sourceGift}} you gave me was a gracious surprise, the kind of kindness that lingers after the moment passes.^I send {{item}} in reply. May it be a useful token of my appreciation.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.ellio: Dear @,^You brought {{sourceGift}}, and in doing so turned an unfinished concern into a completed line.^Please accept {{item}} with my sincere gratitude.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.linus.2: Friend,^Thank you for {{sourceGift}}. A gift freely given carries no demand, only warmth.^I am sending {{item}}. May it be useful, and may you travel lightly today.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.linus: Friend,^You remembered {{sourceGift}}, and that kindness eased the day.^Please accept {{item}}. It is simple, but it is given honestly.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.maru.2: Hi @,^Thanks for {{sourceGift}}. It was a small input with a surprisingly positive effect on the system that was my day.^I sent {{item}}. I think it has good practical potential.
Hi @,^Bringing {{sourceGift}} solved the problem cleanly and saved me from a much messier workaround.^Please take {{item}} as thanks. Consider it the successful output of the process.
gift.mail.body.reciprocal.shane.2: Hey @,^I keep thinking I should say something about {{sourceGift}}, so here it is: thanks. It was good.^I sent {{item}}. That is me being nice, so do not make me regret writing it down.
gift.mail.body.help_request_reward.shane: Hey @,^You helped with {{sourceGift}} and did not make a whole thing out of it. I appreciated that.^There is {{item}} in the letter. Take it before I get weird about gratitude.
gift.mail.body.default: Dear @,^I saw {{item}} and thought you might find a use for it. It felt better to send it now than to wait until I ran into you.^Take care.^- {{npc}}

command.livingnpcs_debug.description: Print the NPC's current state, recent behavior choice reason, help-request fit, and memory recall summary. Usage: livingnpcs_debug [near|NPC name]
command.livingnpcs_prompt.description: Print the full hidden context LivingNPCs will inject into ValleyTalk. Usage: livingnpcs_prompt [near|NPC name]
command.livingnpcs_export.description: Export a Markdown debug report. Usage: livingnpcs_export [near|all|NPC name]
command.livingnpcs_eval.description: Run a lightweight runtime diagnostic suite to check key personality rules.
command.livingnpcs_giftmail.description: Diagnose LivingNPCs gift mail: tracking state, mailbox position, Data/mail entry, generated text, and orphaned dead letters.
command.livingnpcs_forget.description: Clear LivingNPCs behavior memory/state. Usage: livingnpcs_forget [near|NPC name|all confirm]

gmcm.para.loaded: LivingNPCs is loaded. If you can see this page, the mod is running.
gmcm.para.commands: SMAPI debug commands: livingnpcs_debug, livingnpcs_prompt, livingnpcs_export, livingnpcs_eval, livingnpcs_forget.
gmcm.enableMod.name: Enable LivingNPCs
gmcm.enableMod.tooltip: Changes take full effect after a game restart.
gmcm.behaviorHotkey.name: Behavior hotkey
gmcm.behaviorHotkey.tooltip: Press this key near an NPC to trigger one small NPC behavior.
gmcm.inspectMemoryHotkey.name: Inspect-memory hotkey
gmcm.inspectMemoryHotkey.tooltip: Press this key near an NPC to print that NPC's LivingNPCs behavior memory to the SMAPI console.
gmcm.debug.name: Debug logging
gmcm.debug.tooltip: Print more LivingNPCs debug info to the SMAPI console.
gmcm.showHud.name: Show trigger messages
gmcm.showHud.tooltip: After a manual trigger, show the result or failure reason in the bottom-left corner.
gmcm.manualMode.name: Manual behavior mode
gmcm.manualMode.tooltip: Choose which behavior the hotkey always tests. Auto picks one by the rules.
gmcm.manualEmote.name: Manual emote id
gmcm.manualEmote.tooltip: Used when the manual behavior mode is Emote. 16 is usually the exclamation mark.
gmcm.maxMemory.name: Memories kept per NPC
gmcm.maxMemory.tooltip: How many LivingNPCs behavior memories each NPC saves to the save file at most.
gmcm.promptMemory.name: Memories sent to ValleyTalk
gmcm.promptMemory.tooltip: When building context, how many recent behavior memories to send to ValleyTalk at most.
gmcm.conversationMemory.name: Record conversation starts
gmcm.conversationMemory.tooltip: When you click an NPC to chat, record a light interaction memory and send context to ValleyTalk early.
gmcm.helpRequests.name: Enable NPC help requests
gmcm.helpRequests.tooltip: Allow NPCs to ask the player for one light item favor during AI conversation when the relationship feels natural.
gmcm.maxPendingHelp.name: Max pending help requests per NPC
gmcm.maxPendingHelp.tooltip: Limit how many unfinished help requests a single NPC can have at once.
gmcm.helpCooldown.name: Help request cooldown (days)
gmcm.helpCooldown.tooltip: Minimum number of days between two new help requests from the same NPC.
gmcm.helpMinTrust.name: Min trust for help requests
gmcm.helpMinTrust.tooltip: An NPC only opens up to ask for help once relationship trust reaches this value.
gmcm.helpDailyChance.name: Daily help-request chance
gmcm.helpDailyChance.tooltip: When you first talk to an NPC with at least 2 hearts each day, the chance they bring up a small favor on their own. 0 disables proactive asking.
gmcm.helpMinReward.name: Min friendship reward on completion
gmcm.helpMinReward.tooltip: The least extra friendship granted for completing an NPC help request.
gmcm.helpMaxReward.name: Max friendship reward on completion
gmcm.helpMaxReward.tooltip: The most extra friendship granted for completing an NPC help request.
gmcm.aiFriendship.name: AI conversation bonus friendship
gmcm.aiFriendship.tooltip: Let AI conversations grant extra friendship by chat quality, on top of vanilla dialogue friendship, capped per NPC per day.
gmcm.aiFriendshipCap.name: Daily AI bonus friendship cap
gmcm.aiFriendshipCap.tooltip: The most bonus friendship an NPC can gain per day from AI conversation. Vanilla dialogue friendship does not count toward this.
gmcm.dialogueFollowUps.name: Dialogue follow-up bubbles
gmcm.dialogueFollowUps.tooltip: Allow AI conversations to leave a brief follow-up overhead bubble when fitting; companion outings never use it to report movement.
gmcm.dialogueDrivenBehaviors.name: Dialogue-driven behaviors
gmcm.dialogueDrivenBehaviors.tooltip: Allow AI conversations, under strict limits, to influence an NPC's small behaviors over the next minutes to days, such as being more willing to approach, keeping distance, or responding naturally at a mentioned place.
gmcm.behaviorInfluenceDays.name: Max behavior-influence days
gmcm.behaviorInfluenceDays.tooltip: How many days a dialogue-driven behavior influence lasts at most. After it expires it no longer affects the NPC.
gmcm.aiWorldActions.name: Allow AI world actions
gmcm.aiWorldActions.tooltip: Allow AI, under strict limits, to trigger a few real effects such as small gifts, a little money, companion outings, festival moments, or light quest assists.
gmcm.smallGifts.name: Allow NPC small gifts
gmcm.smallGifts.tooltip: Allow NPCs with at least 5 hearts to occasionally give the player a low-value small gift.
gmcm.meaningfulGifts.name: Allow NPC meaningful gifts
gmcm.meaningfulGifts.tooltip: Only with a deep relationship, a recent special event, or a conversation that touches an important long-term memory may an NPC give a more heartfelt gift.
gmcm.meaningfulCooldown.name: Meaningful gift cooldown (days)
gmcm.meaningfulCooldown.tooltip: Minimum days between two meaningful gifts from the same NPC.
gmcm.giftChanceMin.name: 5-heart daily gift chance (min)
gmcm.giftChanceMin.tooltip: After an NPC reaches 5 hearts, the lower bound of the chance to naturally offer a small gift in the first fitting conversation each day.
gmcm.giftChanceMax.name: 5-heart daily gift chance (max)
gmcm.giftChanceMax.tooltip: After an NPC reaches 5 hearts, the upper bound of the chance to naturally offer a small gift in the first fitting conversation each day.
gmcm.moneyGifts.name: Allow NPC money gifts
gmcm.moneyGifts.tooltip: Only when fairly close may an NPC occasionally give the player a little money.
gmcm.moneyCap.name: Max money per gift
gmcm.moneyCap.tooltip: The most gold an AI money gift gives the player at once.
gmcm.companionOutings.name: Allow companion outings
gmcm.companionOutings.tooltip: Allow an NPC to accept an invitation to wander somewhere, enjoy the view, or sit quietly for a while, temporarily stepping away from the day's schedule.
gmcm.outingStay.name: Minimum outing stay
gmcm.outingStay.tooltip: How many game minutes the NPC stays after arriving; default 60 minutes.
gmcm.festival.name: Allow festival interactions
gmcm.festival.tooltip: Allow AI to trigger light special interactions during festivals or events.
gmcm.questAssist.name: Allow quest assistance
gmcm.questAssist.tooltip: Allow AI to lightly assist around the player's current quests, without completing them or changing their state.
gmcm.npcState.name: Enable NPC state
gmcm.npcState.tooltip: Track each NPC's light current state, such as mood, attentiveness, and response leaning.
gmcm.stateDecay.name: Daily state decay
gmcm.stateDecay.tooltip: How far NPC state falls back toward normal at the start of each day.
gmcm.emotionDecay.name: Daily emotion decay
gmcm.emotionDecay.tooltip: How far long-term interpersonal emotion falls back toward calm at the start of each day.
gmcm.conflictDecay.name: Daily conflict decay
gmcm.conflictDecay.tooltip: How far unresolved conflict severity naturally drops at the start of each day; apologies and fitting gifts speed recovery further.
gmcm.passive.name: Passive behaviors
gmcm.passive.tooltip: Allow nearby NPCs to react naturally now and then without pressing the hotkey.
gmcm.passiveChance.name: Passive trigger chance
gmcm.passiveChance.tooltip: The chance to trigger a passive behavior every 10 in-game minutes.
gmcm.maxBehaviors.name: Max behaviors per NPC per day
gmcm.maxBehaviors.tooltip: Limit how many times LivingNPCs can trigger a behavior for each NPC per day.
gmcm.interactionDistance.name: Interaction distance
gmcm.interactionDistance.tooltip: The maximum distance, in tiles, at which LivingNPCs can affect a nearby NPC.
gmcm.allowFace.name: Allow facing the player
gmcm.allowFace.tooltip: Allow NPCs to turn toward the player.
gmcm.allowEmote.name: Allow emotes
gmcm.allowEmote.tooltip: Allow NPCs to show emote bubbles.
gmcm.allowApproach.name: Allow walking over
gmcm.allowApproach.tooltip: Allow NPCs to pathfind a short distance to a safe tile beside the player.
gmcm.sve.name: Enable SVE compatibility
gmcm.sve.tooltip: When off, LivingNPCs no longer uses SVE-specific profiles, expansion progress, or expansion NPC context.
gmcm.bridge.name: Send behavior context to ValleyTalk
gmcm.bridge.tooltip: Let ValleyTalk's dialogue generation take an NPC's recent LivingNPCs behavior into account.
gmcm.concisePrompt.name: Concise prompt context
gmcm.concisePrompt.tooltip: Send ValleyTalk a slimmer hidden context: keeps mood, memory, relationship and situational cues (gifts/help/outings), but drops the repeated world-stage and reply-guidance text to save tokens. The AI's response format is unaffected. Off by default.
gmcm.aiPlanner.name: Enable AI behavior planner
gmcm.aiPlanner.tooltip: Optional. Use an OpenAI-compatible endpoint to choose NPC behavior intents.
gmcm.plannerEndpoint.name: Planner endpoint
gmcm.plannerModel.name: Planner model
gmcm.plannerModel.tooltip: The model name used to choose NPC behavior intents.
gmcm.plannerTimeout.name: Planner timeout
gmcm.plannerTimeout.tooltip: After the AI planner times out, it falls back to rule-based behavior. Unit: seconds.
gmcm.manualMode.approachPlayer: Test only: walk over to player
